title: AuthToken
description: An ed25519-signed, short-lived, audience-bound bearer token a caller presents for transport authentication instead of (or beside) the static X-CHP-Key (chp-v0.2.md section 5, proposal 0027). The signature covers the canonical header {kind, sub, aud, iat, exp, canonicalization}; the caller block is the same self-attested host identity a mandate principal carries. A host verifies the token is internally valid (signature, attestation binds host_id==sub, iat<=now<exp, aud matches) AND authorizes the caller by pinning sub's public key. Any failure is a transport 401, never a governance denial.

sub.description: The caller's host_id (the authenticated subject a valid token binds).

aud.description: The target host_id this token is valid for (audience binding — a token cannot be replayed to another host).

exp.description: ISO-8601 expiry — the token is invalid at or after this time. Short-lived by design (a captured token is useless after exp).

canonicalization.description: The canonicalization scheme for the signed header (e.g. chp-stable-v1).

caller.description: The caller's self-attested identity — the same block a mandate principal carries. The host verifies host_identity binds host_id==sub to public_key, then authorizes by pinning that public_key.
